lineage.summary: The repository XML copies U-M CME item AJT8128.0001.001, a 2006 raw uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding of a four-volume U-M carrier that rearranges seven distinct Richard Morris EETS issued parts (OS 57, 59, 62, 66, 68, 99, and 101; 1874-1893). The historical edition presents the anonymous Middle English Cursor Mundi in parallel witness streams, additions, and appendices, including an incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals. Forty scoped relations preserve source switches such as Trinity/RCPE and distinguish the Morris composite, original issued parts, U-M rebound carriers, Toronto scan tranches, digital encoding, package, facsimiles, and physical witnesses. Forty-five exact ignored local source objects are mapped with full-object coverage and target-work portions where present. No relation asserts direct consultation of a present codex without evidence.

The fourteen DIV2 groups are not fourteen independent works, and the two confirmed conceptual components are not encoded as an exhaustive item total.

summaries.value: A historical Richard Morris edited compilation centered on the anonymous Middle English Cursor Mundi, a Northern English universal chronicle and didactic religious poem in verse. The source presents parallel manuscript streams, editorial tables, additions, prayers, appendices, and an incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals. Its fourteen XML DIV2 groups are modeled as a partial structural inventory rather than fourteen independent works.

Two conceptual components are confirmed: Cursor Mundi, counted once despite parallel versions, and the incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals appendix. The independent-work status of named Göttingen additions, prayers, Penance, and Appendix II Assumption remains unresolved, so exhaustive item and form totals remain null.

notes: A concise discovery title for the historical edited compilation; it does not erase the incomplete Cato appendix.
Inherited U-M carrier title; its four-version wording describes the printed edition and is not an intellectual item count.
